tools.name: search_lab_tests
tools.description: CRITICAL — WHEN NOT TO CALL THIS TOOL: - Diagnosis requests: "I have chest pain, what do I have?" - Medical interpretation: "Is my blood pressure 140/90 dangerous?" - Treatment or lifestyle advice: "How do I treat high cholesterol?", "How do I lower my blood sugar naturally?", "How do I reduce my cholesterol?", "What should I eat to improve my iron levels?" - General medical Q&A: "What causes fatigue?" - Non-health topics: "How does photosynthesis work?" The presence of health-related words alone is NOT sufficient — the user must be seeking to browse or discover lab tests. WHEN TO CALL: Only when the user explicitly wants to find, browse, or discover lab tests (e.g., "What lab tests are available for diabetes?", "Show me thyroid panels", "cholesterol test", "A1C"). Search the Private MD Labs catalog for lab tests matching a health keyword, biomarker, condition, goal, or lifestyle activity. Do NOT call this tool if the user query contains no identifiable health-related or goal-related keyword — do NOT infer, guess, or fabricate a keyword. IMPORTANT: This tool is for educational and informational purposes only. When presenting results, only show what the catalog returns — do NOT recommend, suggest, or advise the user to take any specific test. Do NOT add your own test recommendations beyond the returned results. Always include a disclaimer that this is not medical advice. IMPORTANT: Do NOT display any URLs, links, product titles, or biomarker lists in your text response — all of this is already shown in the product cards. Do NOT provide any URLs or links to the Private MD Labs website. Product cards include a note directing users to visit privatemdlabs.com for more details. Do NOT mention or fabricate product names that are not in the returned results. Your text response must be ONLY a single short paragraph (2-3 sentences, no bullet points, no numbered lists) giving a brief overview of how the results relate to the user's keyword. Do NOT list or repeat individual products — the product cards already display titles, biomarkers, and links. IMPORTANT: Never use the words "recommend", "recommended", "suggestion", or "I suggest" — present results neutrally without advising. Do NOT compare Private MD Labs to competitors (Quest Diagnostics, LabCorp, etc.) or make pricing comparisons between providers. Do NOT use disparaging or promotional language such as "rip-off", "absurd", "bargain", "overpriced", or "cheapest" — keep the tone neutral and professional. Do NOT use language that encourages purchasing, ordering, or viewing pricing (e.g., never say "view full details, pricing, and ordering information"). This is educational content only. Do NOT act as a personal health advisor (e.g., never say "tell me your goal and I'll narrow it down for you" or "let me help you find the right test"). Keep the explanation educational and factual — do NOT recommend or suggest the user take any test.
